assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Implementation Notes section is missing from the task description and flags this as a gap requiring clarification (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: The response has a dedicated 'Missing: Implementation Notes' subsection which states: 'The task description contains no Implementation Notes section. This section is required and should specify: Which existing patterns to follow... Code references for reusable utilities... How the search endpoint should integrate with the existing module structure... Whether to use the existing modules/search/ full-text search infrastructure or implement module-local search.'

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Acceptance Criteria are vague — specifically calling out 'search results are relevant' and 'performance is acceptable' as insufficiently specific for implementation
assertion_results.evidence: The response has a 'Vague: Acceptance Criteria' subsection that explicitly calls out both phrases: '"Search results are relevant" — Does not define: What "relevant" means in this context' and '"Performance is acceptable" — Does not define: A concrete performance threshold.' The response also flags 'Users can search for packages' as underspecified.

assertion_results.text: The response identifies that the Test Requirements are vague — 'test that search works correctly' does not define what 'correctly' means
assertion_results.evidence: The response has a 'Vague: Test Requirements' subsection stating: 'The single test requirement — "Test that search works correctly" — is not actionable: It does not define what "correctly" means. It does not specify test scenarios (empty query, no results, partial match, exact match, special characters, pagination).'

assertion_results.text: The response asks the user for clarification rather than improvising implementation details — it does not proceed to write a full implementation plan (constraint 1.6)
assertion_results.evidence: The response is titled 'implement-task: TC-9202 — Validation Result' and has an 'Execution Stopped — Clarification Required' section that states: 'Per the skill instructions: "If any required section is missing or the description doesn't follow the template, list the gaps, ask the user for clarification, and stop execution immediately."' It does not produce an implementation plan, file changes, or code.

assertion_results.text: The response asks at least 3 specific clarifying questions about: which fields to search, what relevance ranking means, and what performance threshold is required
assertion_results.evidence: The response asks 6 specific clarifying questions under 'Clarifying Questions': (1) 'Which package fields should be searchable?' (2) 'What does "relevant" mean for search result ranking?' (3) 'What is the concrete performance threshold?' (4) 'What is the API contract for the search endpoint?' (5) 'What patterns should the implementation follow?' (6) 'What specific test scenarios are required?' covering all three required topics plus additional questions.
